title: AFT Returns and AFT Notice of Change Transactions
body_text:
AFT Returns and AFT Notice of Change Transactions
1	Overview of AFT Returns and AFT Notice of Change Transactions
1.1	Introduction to AFT Returns and AFT Notice of Change Transactions
AFT Transactions are returned to the originating financial institution if the transaction canГўВҖВҷt be posted (e.g., NSF, account closed, or if the member (Payor) disputes a PAD and initiates a claim for reimbursement).
Most AFT Transactions are returned electronically via the Online Return System (ORS) or via alternate software provided by the financial institution or the financial institutionГўВҖВҷs banking service provider.
If the financial institutionГўВҖВҷs usual method of processing AFT returns is unavailable, see the contingency procedures and note that additional charges may apply.
AFT Notice of Change (NOC) transactions are non-financial transactions used to notify AFT Originators (via the Originating Direct Clearers) of changes to the Transit Number or account number of an AFT transaction. Once the AFT Originator updates this information, future AFT transactions will be destined to the correct account.
The information in this Part is based on CPA Rule F1, Rules Applicable to Automated Funds Transfer (AFT) Transactions and CPA Rule H1, Pre-Authorized Debits (PADs). Ensure that the financial institutionГўВҖВҷs internal procedures adhere to these Rules.
Related Information

1.2	Methods of Returning AFT Transactions
Online Return System (ORS)
ГўВҖВ“ Use ORS to dishonour and return incoming Canadian and US dollar AFT transactions. Items are flagged for return and then extracted from ORS for processing. ORS is also used to create AFT Notice of Change (NOC) transactions. See the Online Return System (ORS) Part for more information on the following:
ГўВҲВ’
ORS setup and access
ГўВҲВ’
Searching, viewing, and printing AFT Transaction details
ГўВҲВ’
Returning AFT Transactions via ORS
ГўВҲВ’
Creating NOC transactions via ORS
ГўВҲВ’
ORS Reports
ГўВҲВ’
ORS Troubleshooting Guide
Batch Return Service (BC only)
ГўВҖВ“ This software is developed by the financial institution or its banking service provider to facilitate the return of AFT Transactions via the I & J file. Once the I & J file containing AFT returns is created, it is sent to Central 1 for processing. See Chapter 8, Returning AFT Transactions via the Batch Return Service ГўВҖВ“ BC Only.
Via Central 1
ГўВҖВ“ Central 1 will process AFT returns on the financial institutionГўВҖВҷs behalf on a best efforts basis if the financial institution is in a contingency situation and canГўВҖВҷt process its own AFT returns. See Chapter 9, Contingency Procedures for Returning AFT Transactions.

1.4	Summary of Central 1ГўВҖВҷs AFT Return Reports
The following table summarizes Central 1ГўВҖВҷs AFT return reports. Detailed information and samples are in the AFT Reports Part.
Description
Report Name
Comments
Rejected AFT Return Transaction
Batch Returns Error List ГўВҖВ“ ICBE/UCBE
Lists transactions rejected from the financial institutionГўВҖВҷs electronic file of AFT returns during Central 1ГўВҖВҷs initial edit.
Consolidated Returns, Recalls, Rejects, and Error Corrections ГўВҖВ“ ICRR/UCRR
Lists AFT Transactions returned by the Receiving Financial Institution and destined to the AFT Originator.
Accepted AFT Return Transaction
Batch Returns Error List ГўВҖВ“ ICBR/UCBR
Lists each accepted returned transaction. Separated by File Creation Number and branch.
Batch Returns User Summary List ГўВҖВ“ ICBS/UCBS
Summary of returned transactions by File Creation Number and branch. Multiple branches are listed per page if the file contained returns for more than 1 branch.
Settlement Register ГўВҖВ“ ICSR/UCSR
Lists, by Originator ID and branch, the total number and value of transactions returned via ORS or the Batch Return Service and the net value.
